revision.text: #redirect[[en:甲]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''甲''' *（こう、きのえ）十干の一つ。本項で詳述する。 *（こう）[[甲斐国]]の略称。甲州。 *（こう）[[甲羅]]のこと。 *（こう）[[手]]や[[足]]の面の一つ。指紋がなく毛が生えている方。手の甲、足の甲。 *（かん）十干の甲（こう）が転じたもので、雅楽などにおける高い音域のこと。「甲高い（かんだかい）」はここからできた言葉である。 ---- '''甲'''（こう、きのえ）は、[[十干]]の一番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[木]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「きのえ」（木の兄）ともいう。
甲の字は亀の甲羅を形取ったもので、そこから、甲羅のような堅い殻に覆われた[[種]]の状態の意味として、十干の一番目に宛てられた。
==甲を含む干支== *[[甲子]] *[[甲戌]] *[[甲申]] *[[甲午]] *[[甲辰]] *[[甲寅]]
{{stub}} [[Category:十干|きのえ]]

甲の字は亀の甲羅を形取ったもので、そこから、甲羅のような堅い殻に覆われた[[種]]の状態の意味として、十干の一番目に宛てられた。
==甲を含む干支== *[[甲子]] *[[甲戌]] *[[甲申]] *[[甲午]] *[[甲辰]] *[[甲寅]]

'''甲''' #（こう、きのえ）十干の一つ。本項で詳述する。 #（こう）[[甲斐国]]の略称。甲州。 #（こう）[[甲羅]]のこと。 #（こう）[[手]]や[[足]]の面の一つ。指紋がなく毛が生えている方。手の甲、足の甲。 #（こう）中国の[[明]]・[[清]]代に行なわれていた[[里甲制]]における一組織。 #（こう）[[琵琶]]・[[三味線]]などの弦楽器の胴。 #（こう）[[冠]]の広くて平たい部分。 #（かん）十干の甲（こう）が転じたもので、雅楽などにおける高い音域のこと。「甲高い（かんだかい）」はここからできた言葉である。 ---- {{十干}} '''甲'''（こう、きのえ）は、[[十干]]の一番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[木]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「きのえ」（木の兄）ともいう。
甲の字は[[亀]]の甲羅を形取ったもので、そこから、甲羅のような堅い殻に覆われた[[種]]の状態の意味として、十干の一番目に宛てられた。
==甲を含む干支== *[[甲子]] *[[甲戌]] *[[甲申]] *[[甲午]] *[[甲辰]] *[[甲寅]]
{{stub}} [[Category:十干|きのえ]]

'''甲''' #（こう、きのえ）十干の一つ。本項で詳述する。 #（こう）[[甲斐国]]の略称。甲州。 #（こう）[[甲羅]]のこと。 #（こう）[[手]]や[[足]]の面の一つ。指紋がなく毛が生えている方。手の甲、足の甲。 #（こう）中国の[[明]]・[[清]]代に行なわれていた[[里甲制]]における一組織。 #（こう）台湾で使用されている面積の単位（[[:zh:台灣甲|台灣甲]]） #（こう）[[琵琶]]・[[三味線]]などの弦楽器の胴。 #（こう）[[冠]]の広くて平たい部分。 #（かん）十干の甲（こう）が転じたもので、雅楽などにおける高い音域のこと。「甲高い（かんだかい）」はここからできた言葉である。 ---- {{十干}} '''甲'''（こう、きのえ）は、[[十干]]の一番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[木]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「きのえ」（木の兄）ともいう。
甲の字は[[亀]]の甲羅を形取ったもので、そこから、甲羅のような堅い殻に覆われた[[種]]の状態の意味として、十干の一番目に宛てられた。
十干を順位づけに使った場合には、甲は1番目の意味となる。また、分類の名称として「甲類」「甲種」などと言うことがあるが、これは必ずしも順位を示すものではない。
==甲を含む干支== *[[甲子]] *[[甲戌]] *[[甲申]] *[[甲午]] *[[甲辰]] *[[甲寅]]

甲の字は[[亀]]の甲羅を形取ったもので、そこから、甲羅のような堅い殻に覆われた[[種子|種]]の状態の意味として、十干の一番目に宛てられた。

[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[メタン]]、[[メチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を1つ含む化合物や[[官能基]]に付けられる。ただし、[[ビタミン]]などではAの代わりに使われている。

{{theotheruses|十干の一つ}} {{十干}} '''甲'''（こう、きのえ）は、[[十干]]の1番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[木]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「きのえ」（木の兄）ともいう。
甲の字は[[亀]]の甲羅を形取ったもので、そこから、甲羅のような堅い殻に覆われた[[種子|種]]の状態の意味として、十干の一番目に宛てられた。
十干を順位づけに使った場合には、甲は1番目の意味となる。また、分類の名称として「甲類」「甲種」などと言うことがあるが、これは必ずしも順位を示すものではない。
[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[メタン]]、[[メチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を1つ含む化合物や[[官能基]]に付けられる。ただし、[[ビタミン]]などではAの代わりに使われている。
==甲を含む干支== *[[甲子]] *[[甲戌]] *[[甲申]] *[[甲午]] *[[甲辰]] *[[甲寅]]
{{stub}} [[Category:干支|きのえ]]
[[zh:甲 (天干)]]

{{theotheruses|十干の一つ}} {{十干}} '''甲'''（こう、きのえ）は、[[十干]]の1番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[木]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「きのえ」（木の兄）ともいう。

十干を順位づけに使った場合には、甲は1番目の意味となる。また、分類の名称として「甲類」「甲種」などと言うことがあるが、これは必ずしも順位を示すものではない。
甲の年は、[[西暦]]年の下一桁が4の年が甲の[[年]]となる。
[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[メタン]]、[[メチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を1つ含む化合物や[[官能基]]に付けられる。ただし、[[ビタミン]]などではAの代わりに使われている。
==甲を含む干支== *[[甲子]] *[[甲戌]] *[[甲申]] *[[甲午]] *[[甲辰]] *[[甲寅]]
{{stub}} [[Category:干支|きのえ]]
[[zh:甲 (天干)]]

「[[中国語]]の[[ビタミン]]などではAの代わりに使われている。」
そういう表現はありません。日本語と同じようにA、B、C、Dなどを使っています。

{{Otheruses|十干の一つ}} {{十干十二支}} '''甲'''（こう、きのえ）は、[[十干]]の1番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[木]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「きのえ」（木の兄）ともいう。
甲の字は[[カメ|亀]]の甲羅を形取ったもので、そこから、甲羅のような堅い殻に覆われた[[種子|種]]の状態の意味として、十干の一番目に宛てられた。
十干を順位づけに使った場合には、甲は1番目の意味となる。また、分類の名称として「甲類」「甲種」などと言うことがあるが、これは必ずしも順位を示すものではない。
[[西暦]]年の下一桁が4の年が甲の[[年]]となる。
[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[メタン]]、[[メチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を1つ含む化合物や[[官能基]]に付けられる。
== 甲を含む干支 == {{Wiktionary|甲}} * [[甲子]] * [[甲戌]] * [[甲申]] * [[甲午]] * [[甲辰]] * [[甲寅]]
{{Culture-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:きのえ}} [[Category:干支]]

十干を順位づけに使った場合には、甲は1番目の意味となる。また、分類の名称として「甲類」「甲種」などと言うことがあるが、これは必ずしも順位を示すものではない。

[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[メタン]]、[[メチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を1つ含む化合物や[[官能基]]に付けられる。

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}}'''Kō''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Ten Heavenly Stems|ja|十干}}.
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=<nowiki>Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ]</nowiki> |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Ten Heavenly Stems]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kō == {{Wiktionary|甲}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=Yang Wood Rat (甲子)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=Yang Wood Dog (甲戌)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=Yang Wood Monkey (甲申)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=Yang Wood Horse (甲午)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=Yang Wood Dragon (甲辰)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=Yang Wood Tiger (甲寅)|lt_ko=갑인}} |} *{{DEFAULTSORT:Kinoye}} [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:Ten Heavenly Stems]]
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== 出典 === {{Reflist}} *
{{DEFAULTSORT:きのえ}}

According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=<nowiki>Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ]</nowiki> |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).

Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=<nowiki>Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ]</nowiki> |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Zodiac Combinations with Kō == {{Wiktionary|甲}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=Yang Wood Rat (甲子)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=Yang Wood Dog (甲戌)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=Yang Wood Monkey (甲申)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=Yang Wood Horse (甲午)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=Yang Wood Dragon (甲辰)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=Yang Wood Tiger (甲寅)|lt_ko=갑인}} |} *{{DEFAULTSORT:Kinoye}} [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:Ten Heavenly Stems]]

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}}'''Kō''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=<nowiki>Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ]</nowiki> |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Ten Heavenly Stems]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kō == {{Wiktionary|甲}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=Yang Wood Rat (甲子)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=Yang Wood Dog (甲戌)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=Yang Wood Monkey (甲申)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=Yang Wood Horse (甲午)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=Yang Wood Dragon (甲辰)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:center;font-size:80%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=Yang Wood Tiger (甲寅)|lt_ko=갑인}} |} *{{DEFAULTSORT:Kinoye}} [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:Ten Heavenly Stems]]

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인}} |}

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인}} |}

== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{Otheruseslist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인}} |}

Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인}} |}
*{{citation needed:Kinoye}} [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}}

{{citation neededlist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인}} |}

{{citation neededlist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Five Elements Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]

{{citation neededlist|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]

According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인}} |}

Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]][[ja:甲]]

revision.text:
{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill| Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=[[Category:nowiki former opening]]Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ][[Category:nowiki former closing]] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[ja:甲]]
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인}} |}
*{{citation needed|Kinoye}} [[Category:Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]]*

Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en|12=draft}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木|12=draft}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=draft}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=draft}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=draft}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=draft}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=draft}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=draft}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=draft}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=draft}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン|12=draft}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基|12=draft}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=draft}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인|12=draft}} |}
*{{citation needed|Kinoye}} *

According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木|12=draft}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=draft}}" (elder wood).

Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=draft}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=draft}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=draft}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=draft}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=draft}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=draft}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=draft}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン|12=draft}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基|12=draft}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=draft}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>

Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=draft|12=draft}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=draft|12=draft}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. {{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=draft|12=draft}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=draft|12=draft}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=draft|12=draft}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=draft|12=draft}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=draft|12=draft}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=draft|12=draft}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=draft|12=draft}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=draft|12=draft}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인|12=draft|12=draft}} |}

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=draft|12=draft}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=draft|12=draft}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=draft|12=draft}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=draft|12=draft}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=draft|12=draft}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=draft|12=draft}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=draft|12=draft}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=draft|12=draft}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인|12=draft|12=draft}} |}
*{{citation needed|Kinoye}} *
