{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=draft|12=draft}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=draft|12=draft}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=draft|12=draft}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=draft|12=draft}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=draft|12=draft}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=draft|12=draft}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=draft|12=draft}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=draft|12=draft}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Wood Rat|ja|甲子|zh|甲子|ko|갑자|fr|Rat de bois|de|Holz-Ratte|en|Wood Rat|lt=甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)|lt_ko=갑자|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dog|ja|甲戌|zh|甲戌|ko|갑술|fr|Chien de bois|de|Holz-Hund|en|Wood Dog|lt=甲戌 (Yang Wood Dog)|lt_ko=갑술|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Monkey|ja|甲申|zh|甲申|ko|갑신|fr|Singe de bois|de|Holz-Affe|en|Wood Monkey|lt=甲申 (Yang Wood Monkey)|lt_ko=갑신|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|ja|甲午|zh|甲午|ko|갑오|fr|Cheval de bois|de|Holz-Pferd|en|Wood Horse|lt=甲午 (Yang Wood Horse)|lt_ko=갑오|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|甲辰|zh|甲辰|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de bois|de|Holz-Drache|en|Wood Dragon|lt=甲辰 (Yang Wood Dragon)|lt_ko=갑진|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|甲寅|zh|甲寅|ko|갑인|fr|Tigre de bois|de|Holz-Tiger|en|Wood Tiger|lt=甲寅 (Yang Wood Tiger)|lt_ko=갑인|12=draft|12=draft}} |}

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]}}{{十干十二支}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|en|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|lt=wood element|ja|木|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=draft|12=draft}}" (elder wood).
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * When used for ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=draft|12=draft}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=draft|12=draft}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in rank. * In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=draft|12=draft}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=draft|12=draft}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=draft|12=draft}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=draft|12=draft}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|methane|ja|メタン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|methyl group|ja|メチル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=draft|12=draft}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

revision.text:
{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]|qq=}}{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|lt=wood element|ja|Wood (木)|1=Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|lt=Wood (木)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (木)|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|13=User:Immanuelle/Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ hành)|20=tr|21=Tahta (klasik element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|qq=}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|qq=}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |[[:en:Wood Rat|甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)]] |- Horse)|lt_ko=갑오|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Horse|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Horse|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Horse|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

(甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]|qq=}}{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang == Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

{{citation needed|'''The first of the Ten Heavenly Stems'''|other uses|[[Ko (disambiguation)]]|qq=}}{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kinoe''' (甲) is the first of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>[https://skdesu.com/en/meaning/%E7%94%B2-kinoe/ 甲 | kinoe - Meaning in Japanese]</ref><ref>[https://www.tanoshiijapanese.com/dictionary/entry_details.cfm?entry_id=55812 <nowiki>Entry Details for 甲 [kou] - Tanoshii Japanese</nowiki>]</ref>
According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}}, it is associated with the yang aspect of the {{ill|Wood (wuxing)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|2=uk|3=Дерево (елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |[[:en:Wood Rat|甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)]] |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood element)|22=ta|23=மரம் (சீன மெய்யியல்)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |[[:en:Wood Rat|甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)]] |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Horse|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Wood Horse|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|1=Yang Wood Dragon|2=ja|甲辰|1=Yang Wood link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Tiger|ja|1=Yang Wood Tiger|2=ja|甲寅|1=Yang Wood Tiger|2=ja|3=甲寅|zh|1=Yang Wood Tiger|2=ja|甲寅|1=Yang Wood is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky == Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

(甲)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
