== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

(elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
== Zodiac Combinations with Kinoe (甲) == {{Wiktionary|甲}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |[[:en:Wood Rat|甲子 (Yang Wood Rat)]] |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Wood Dragon|ja|1=Yang Wood Dragon|2=ja|甲辰|1=Yang Wood Dragon|zh|1=Yang Wood Dragon|2=ja|甲辰|1=Yang Wood Dragon|ko|갑진|fr|Dragon de == Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

(елемент)|4=id|5=Kayu (Wu Xing)|6=ms|7=Kayu (Wu Xing)|8=bcl|9=Kahoy (wuxing)|10=ca|11=Fusta (wuxing)|12=it|13=Legno (elemento)|14=es|15=Madera (Wu Xing)|16=nl|17=Hout (element)|18=vi|19=Mộc (Ngũ == Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

(甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

(elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (木)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and in Japan, it is referred to as "{{ill|Kinoe (甲)|kinoe|ja|きのえ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (elder wood).
== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]], years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as [[:en:Methane|methane]] and [[:en:Methyl group|methyl group]]. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|Methane|en|Methane|WD=Q37129|lt=methane}} and {{ill|Methyl group|en|Methyl group|WD=Q4407|lt=methyl group}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Pronunciations: 甲 (Stem 1) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jiǎ * Korean: Gap (갑) * Vietnamese: Giáp * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoe (きのえ, 木の兄 – “Elder Wood”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "甲" has been the subject of various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=于省吾| year=1996| title=甲骨文字誁林| location=Beijing| publisher=中華書局| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=3582–6|qq=}}</ref> Its use as a term to denote the first of the Ten Heavenly Stems is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * When used for Ranking, "Kō" signifies the first position. Additionally, it is used in classification names such as "{{ill|Kōrui|ja|甲類|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrui|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" and "{{ill|Kōshu|ja|甲種|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}," though these do not necessarily indicate superiority in Rank. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}}, years ending with the digit 4 are considered "Kō" years. The alternative name for the year is "{{ill|Afu|ja|閼逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Afu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (also written as "{{ill|Appō|ja|閼蓬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Appō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" or "{{ill|Enpō|ja|焉逢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}").<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dictionary Entry [閼逢: アツホウ] |url=https://dict.revised.moe.edu.tw/dictView.jsp?ID=147004&la=0&powerMode=0 |website=Ministry of Education Revised Chinese Dictionary 2021 |access-date=2024-03-08 |language=zh-Hant-TW|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to IUPAC命名法|1=IUPAC nomenclature of chemistry|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20826683}}, it is used to denote compounds and {{ill|functional groups|ja|官能基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/functional groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 基|1=functional group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q170409}} containing one carbon atom, such as {{ill|Methane|en|Methane|WD=Q37129|lt=methane}} and {{ill|Methyl group|en|Methyl group|WD=Q4407|lt=methyl group}}. * Its opposite counterpart is {{ill|Ki (己)|ja|己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|lt=Tsuchinoto (己)|2=ja|3=己|4=zh|5=己|1=Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki (己)|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinoto (己)|qq=}} (tsuchinoto). * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} associated with Kō is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).
